what_it_does: Declares a generated desktop surface specification for view D2 in area D, anchored to journey J3, listing the UI addresses it surfaces and the components, lint gate, and token source used to build it.
observations: It is a generated surface-spec for view D2 under area D with home journey J3.
It represents WIRE-dispatch, WIRE-review, and GOV-tiers.
It targets desktop only and is in planned status.
It references four ui:// addresses across activity, board, and inbox components.
It defines a build_to section with components, a design_lint command, and a tokens-to-CSS pipeline.
cross_journeys is empty and journeys contains only the home journey J3.
standouts: The title encodes an explicit approve→dispatch→implemented→review workflow.
It binds governance concept GOV-tiers alongside WIRE-dispatch and WIRE-review.
The tokens field asserts a single look-source: design/_system/tokens.json → design-system.css.
conventions: Uses an id/area/title/status/platform structure typical of a blueprint surface spec.
References journeys and cross_journeys as address-ref carriers.
Lists concrete ui:// addresses rather than inventing them.
Separates build concerns into components, lint gate, and token pipeline.
concerns: The design_lint value contains an incomplete command fragment '--fail-on  (the FORM gate)' with a trailing space and placeholder.
Two addresses both live under ui://inbox (build-result and build-review), which may overlap semantically.
Status is planned while it already declares build targets and addresses.
notes: This is a planned desktop surface spec for the build-review flow under journey J3, tying governance tiers to activity, board, and inbox addresses.
questions: What does GOV-tiers concretely require from this surface?
What is the intended distinction between ui://inbox/build-result and ui://inbox/build-review?
Which exact failure modes does the FORM gate enforce?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches: it records a generated surface specification for D2, anchored to journey J3, and enumerates the addresses and build artifacts it surfaces.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a desktop build-approval surface that wires dispatch and review stages together under governance tiers, while keeping visual design governed by a single token source and build quality enforced by a lint gate.
novelty: The title is a directed workflow arrow (approve→dispatch→implemented→review) rather than a simple label, and the tokens field explicitly declares a single CSS look-source.
contribution: It contributes a concrete desktop surface definition for the D2 build-review view, including its journey anchor, address map across activity/board/inbox, represented workflow concepts, and build-time lint and token sourcing requirements.
summary_for_embedding: D2.json is a planned desktop surface specification for area D view D2, home journey J3, representing WIRE-dispatch, WIRE-review, and GOV-tiers, with four ui:// addresses and build targets for activity, board, and inbox.
intention_for_embedding: This file was generated to record which UI addresses and build artifacts belong to the desktop build-approval/dispatch/review surface under journey J3.
